methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $7M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Navier is a seed-stage transportation company founded by Sampriti Bhattacharyya. The company has raised $7.2M to date. The transportation sector contains five private comparables (Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, REGENT Craft) and no public traded peers available for direct valuation comparison.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable method here. Using total capital raised ($7.2M) as a proxy for seed-stage valuation and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier to the next funding stage yields an implied valuation of $18M. A peer-median approach could not be applied: while five transportation companies exist in the broader comp universe, filtering for same-stage or similar-valuation-tier peers yielded insufficient overlap (fewer than three datapoints), making sector-wide median comparison unreliable and potentially misleading.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $15M to $21M, with a median of $18M. This range reflects the single methodological pillar available and the inherent uncertainty of stage-progression mechanics in early-stage venture. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.5x multiplier to Navier's specific risk profile, the extent to which the five transportation comparables reflect market conditions relevant to this seed-stage company, and the absence of public market anchors to validate private-market multiples in the sector.
